title: Luffy Lands in Wano and Meets Tama
description: On pages 23–26, this part focuses on Unknown person #20, Unknown person #21, Unknown person #22, Unknown person #23 as Luffy lands in Wano, meets Tama, and immediately collides with the poverty and cruelty of Kaido’s rule.
scene: Luffy washes up in Wano, meets Tama, and discovers a country suffering under Kaido’s rule.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It can't be! What is this delicious- Ness?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This violently surprising richness! The aromatics del- Icately heighten that rushing tide of flavor!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The lightly heated tomallley has been steeped into the meat, enveloping it in further depth!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: And... what is this?! This lobster is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: As I thought! You've heard of it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: The Tomahawk Spiny Lobster.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: TOMAY SPINY LOSSISTER adapted to both fresh and saltwater habitats. This is a very-rare species of crustacean. It appendages pincers. Instead, sit razor-sharp on the shape of waits, hidden, in with slit deposits, as by using these to

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #21
dialogue.text: Tomahawk Spiny Lobster

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Its front appendages have no pincers. Instead, they boast razor-sharp blades[in the shape of taxes. It waits, hidden, in Rivermouth,sit deposits, and feeds by using these blades to slice and dice passing fish.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Its incredibly tough carapace has been used in Wartime armor, and, in certain folk traditions, in pre-war victory rituals.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #21
dialogue.text: IT CANNOT BE! IM- POSSI- BLE!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This lobster... among the countless in- gredients in this world, it is one of the most difficult to prepare and cook!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The first- rate chefs of famed, top- class restau- rants would struggle to accomplish such a feat!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The rest of the dishes I've eaten here are third-rate at best... a mere waiter at such an establish- ment could never produce a dish like this!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to be honest, I'm not actually a waiter.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #22
dialogue.text: What did you say?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Our part-time waiters all scampered. It happens a lot here, I'm afraid.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So the cooks cover the hall in turns.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the sous chef of the floating restaurant, baratie!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #22
dialogue.text: The name's Sanji! Remember it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #22
dialogue.text: Sous Chef?! You?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Listen here. We can make the "sumptuous" dishes you called for earlier if we so choose to.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But that's not why we run this place.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To provide a moment of respite for travelers on long, rough journeys.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to answer the hunger of the less-than-fortunate that wash up on our decks.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those are the reasons this restaurant exists! It

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Your loose tongue ain't fit to even speak of this res- Taurant!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #23
dialogue.text: Hmph.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #23
dialogue.text: Now, then, miss...

summary: This section advances the Wano Country Arrival storyline with 29 dialogue beats. Luffy’s first steps in Wano establish Tama’s kindness, the country’s hunger, and the brutality of Kaido’s forces.
